title: Block 44 — EventsPage auto-filters by currentSport from SportContext

requirements.summary: Import React hooks and SportContext into EventsPage to consume sportStatus and currentSport
acceptanceCriteria: EventsPage imports useEffect, useState, and useSport
The component calls useSport() and destructures sportStatus and currentSport

file_operations.description: Add imports for useEffect and useState from 'react', and import useSport from '../context/SportContext'. Inside the EventsPage component, call const { sportStatus, currentSport } = useSport() to access sport context state.

requirements.summary: Add useEffect to sync selectedSport with currentSport when sport is active, resetting to 'All' when inactive
acceptanceCriteria: When sportStatus becomes 'active' and currentSport is set, selectedSport automatically updates to match currentSport
When sportStatus is 'inactive' or currentSport is falsy, selectedSport resets to 'All'
The useEffect re-runs whenever sportStatus or currentSport changes

file_operations.description: Add a useEffect hook that observes [sportStatus, currentSport]. When sportStatus === 'active' && currentSport is truthy, call setSelectedSport(currentSport). Otherwise, call setSelectedSport('All'). This ensures the filter auto-updates when the user activates or deactivates a sport.

requirements.summary: Derive filteredEvents list from events array based on selectedSport and render it in the UI
acceptanceCriteria: When selectedSport is 'All', all events are displayed
When selectedSport is a specific sport (e.g. 'Tennis'), only events with event.sport === 'Tennis' are displayed
The event list UI renders from filteredEvents, not the raw events array

file_operations.description: Before rendering the event list, derive filteredEvents by checking: if selectedSport === 'All', set filteredEvents = events; otherwise, set filteredEvents = events.filter(e => e.sport === selectedSport). Replace all references to the 'events' array in the render section with 'filteredEvents' so only matching events are displayed.

requirements.summary: Verify end-to-end auto-filter behavior when activating, deactivating, and manually selecting sport filters
acceptanceCriteria: Deactivating sport shows all events in EventsPage
Activating a sport auto-filters EventsPage to only that sport's events
Tapping the 'All' filter chip manually shows all events regardless of sport status
Deactivating sport after any filter state resets display to all events

file_operations.description: Ensure that the existing manual filter chip UI remains functional and that the useEffect-driven state changes do not remove user control. Test the following flows: (1) deactivate sport → verify selectedSport becomes 'All' and all events render; (2) activate a sport (e.g. Tennis) → verify selectedSport becomes 'Tennis' and only Tennis events render; (3) manually tap 'All' chip → verify all events render; (4) deactivate after manual selection → verify selectedSport resets to 'All' and all events render. Preserve all existing filter chip onClick handlers.
